candidates.stmt: David grew up in Ipswich. He is now retired and able to enjoy the best of Ipswich. He is an active member of the local community as a governor of a Ipswich Primary school, a trustee of the Phoenix Project (a mental health charity) and collecting at Portman Road for Fans Supporting FoodBanks. He is also a very happy ITFC season ticket holder. "The climate should be at the centre of all of our policies. I am excited about tackling local issues within the Alexandra ward and providing a vision for the future of Ipswich."

I am not a yes-man—I will challenge poor decisions and always put Ipswich first. With 37 years of experience building businesses globally, I bring real-world solutions, not politics. I will be a voice for you—fighting for Ipswich to grow, supporting local businesses, and making sure your concerns are heard. I will fight for a cleaner, safer, and more welcoming town we can all be proud of. I will hold authorities accountable on crime and anti-social behaviour, and I will cut wasteful spending at both Borough and County level—reinvesting that money back into our community.

"Paul Anderson is the Labour Party candidate for Bixley at the Borough Council election on Thursday 2nd May 2019.

Paul is a University lecturer and writer who lives in Woodbridge Road. He grew up in Dorchester Road in Bixley in the 1960s and 1970s and went to Britannia primary – and his mum still lives in the ward. He’s Bixley to his bones: he used to buy sweets from ITFC legend John Elsworthy’s shop at the roundabout and took the number 4 bus into town.

Paul knows what Bixley residents need from the council – decent bus services, reliable rubbish collection, better social services support for old people, protection of the heaths from housing development – and will campaign for all that and more on the Borough Council."

Being a Green IBC candidate comes naturally to Stephanie, flowing from a life long concern with the growing pressure on our natural world and the consequent issues which the younger community will inherit if we don’t do more to slow this pressure down.   "We can do better and we can all do more! But we do need our representatives in local and national government to help make this happen.    I’ve run my own business in organic whole foods and environmentally friendly products, I’m a member of Spring round allotment field and I believe that strengthening feelings of community can help us achieve a more sustainable future. So vote Green and we can make a better future."

KATHERINE WEST – A STRONG VOICE FOR RESIDENTS IN ALEXANDRA
Ipswich has always been my home town and I love living and working here with my husband and two children. We all have a vested interest in making sure our town is a safe, enjoyable place to live & raise a family & if elected will fight for this  
I know residents are worried about rising crime & antisocial behaviour, if we want to see improvements tough decisions must be taken & this Council is not doing enough. I listen to concerns about housing, lack of opportunity & parking issues and I share your frustrations. I will be a strong opposition voice on the Council, making sure your views are listened to.
I will support community projects in the area & ensure we get a fair share of available funding. Historically Alexandra hasn't benefited as much as other arts of the town an I would like to reverse this trend.
Another Conservative voice on the Borough Council would allow an even more robust challenge to the current Labour administration. It is only by speaking up and working together that we can make positive changes to ensure the best for this part of town and make it a better place to live and work.
YOUR PRIORITIES ARE MY PRIORITIES
PARKING ENFORCEMENT 
Did you know that parking enforcement is totally the responsibility of IBC yet they consistently fail to enforce the rules in our community? The changes to the Resident’s Parking Scheme has also caused many of you problems. It’s time for a change.
RUBBISH! 
The environment is important to us all so why does this Council fail so badly in our area? Rats in the Town Centre, rubbish bags left out all night, flytipping, graffiti & worst of all landowners allowed to let their land become an eyesore. You don’t deserve this.
AIR QUALITY 
Poor Air Quality is responsible for over 60 deaths in Ipswich each year. IBC are responsible for this in Ipswich and they are failing you. 40% of the worst areas in Ipswich are in Alexandra. Conservative Councillors rece

STEPHEN ION – A STRONG VOICE FOR RESIDENTS IN RUSHMERE
Stephen lives in north east Ipswich and has been your Councillor for the last four years.
During that time he has stood up for residents' views against the Labour run council – most notably when over 250 local people opposed the council's own controversial planning application in Sidegate Lane. He is determined that residents' opinions be heard at planning meetings.
The other Councillors in Rushmere represent the Labour party. If re-elected in May, Stephen will be able to continue to be a Conservative voice holding them to account.
Stephen supports the Ipswich Northern Route and is pleased that all local councils have signed up to the principle.
He also believes that Ipswich has great potential if money is spent in the right areas. Under the Conservatives we can achieve that vision.
Please give Stephen your support on 2 May.
YOUR PRIORITIES ARE MY PRIORITIES
STANDING UP FOR RESIDENTS 
When over 250 local residents voiced their opposition to a controversial planning application in Sidegate Lane, I was the only local Councillor to stand up for their interests and speak against the plans at the planning meeting. I am the only non-Labour Councillor in the ward, without my presence who will stand up for you?
NEW TREES IN WESTBURY ROAD 
It's taken many years of battling with the County Council to get replacement tress for those cut down but Stephen was pleased to eventually get 4 new trees planted late last year. Being a Councillor is not all quick wins, sometimes you have to persevere over a long time to get a result for residents.
GETTING THINGS DONE… 
From helping with funding for new tennis courts, getting weeds and paths cleared, stopping unwanted back garden developments, getting a new wet room for a disabled resident a Councillors life is certainly varied. If re-elected I hope to be able to help many other residents with there day to day issues – be they big or small.

Born in Sweden, Martin moved around a lot in his childhood as his father was a civil engineer.

After attending London University, Martin moved to Ipswich, where he has lived for some 34 years.

Martin has been, successively, a Young Liberal, an SDP student and an Ipswich Borough Councillor for the Liberal Democrats in the 1990s.

After a break from politics, Martin is now back after the events of recent years:

"I just couldn't believe that people are swallowing Tory lies or that the media is either cheering the liars on or being cowed into silence. It's time for everyone to stand up and be counted, in every sense!"

His major interest areas are the environment and transport.

LIZ HARSANT – A STRONG VOICE FOR RESIDENTS IN HOLYWELLS
Liz Harsant has been a resident of Ipswich for many years and a Councilor for 17. In this time, she has been leader of Ipswich Borough Council and worked timelessly to empower residents to improve their neighbourhoods and lives.
She has been instrumental in the Waterfront Regeneration project; The Suffolk University build and the renovation of Holywells Park. Most recently working with the Rivers Action Group to represent residents opposed to the Upper Orwell Crossing which was retracted.
Liz has secured funding to help refurbish the facilities of Patrick’s Boxing Club in York Road, a club that helps young adults to spend time getting fit, active and off the streets. Liz would like Ipswich Borough Council to recognise Patrick’s as a great community asset and other facilities in Ipswich that also serve the community. ‘More facilities like Patrick’s can be the lifeblood for younger adults and help reduce antisocial behaviour. We need more funding from our Borough to provide these vital services,’ explains Liz.
Liz is a compassionate, dedicated Councilor and if re-elected she will be working to: • Tackle antisocial behavior. • Combat parking and traffic congestion especially due to the closing of the Orwell Bridge in extreme winds. • Improve mental health services by working to reduce waiting times for residents, so they can access support services quickly.
“Improving services and facilities will combat antisocial behavior and improve the neighborhoods we all live in. I want to empower residents and communities to live well and be safe in our town,’ said Liz.
YOUR PRIORITIES ARE MY PRIORITIES
RESIDENTS FIRST, PARTY SECOND 
Liz has always put the interests of her residents first, even when it has clashed with her party's views. She worked with the Rivers Action Group representing residents opposed to the Upper Orwell Crossing, which helped result in the scheme's cancellation.
BOXING CLEVER! 
Liz has worked with Pat

I'm Daniel Davey, I was born and raised in Ipswich. I've had the town in my heart throughout my working career in the pub and leisure industry. In my 4 years mananging and running pubs & bars, I've seen and been friends with people from across the spectrum of industries and upbringings. With this experience the one thing I can tell you is finding the right education and mentors is the true key to success.

I developed my interest in politics during 2010, finding it fascinating that two parties came together for a more balanced government. Though on my journey I discovered many within the LibDems felt this as a betrayal within the party, as a new steward looking in, I would hate to think what might have happened if they had not tempered their partners in power at this critical juncture in British history, especially looking back now what has happened without that calming moral presence.

I now feel the world and more importantly, locally, that calming voice is needed again. We have become fractured. It's time to turn that tide back to unity and cooperation. Time to work for long term success instead of short-term one-upmanship that blight the larger parties. As the old adage goes, be the change you wish to see, that is why I am running and that is why I also think you should not ignore the local elections this year. Who you vote for on the 2nd May won't affect the Brexit mess but will affect what happens to your local community and to Ipswich in the coming years. One of the councillors' key  roles is being your first point of contact & to represent you if you have issues and concerns in your local community and I hope to be that voice and man of action for you in the Whitton Ward. Should you wish to contact me, please email me at info@ipswichlibdems.org.uk

I was born and raised in Ipswich, attending Rose Hill Primary School in Holywells ward. Alongside my day job working for a veterans charity, I stay actively involved in our local community as a Friend of Holywells Park and a Governor at Cliff Lane Primary School.

I’m standing for re-election because I care deeply about Ipswich, and the community spaces and public services that help us thrive as a town.

As your Borough Councillor, I’ve worked hard to deliver tangible results: a brand-new playground at Holywells Park for families to enjoy, new high-quality affordable homes on Fore Hamlet, and strong advocacy for better road maintenance where residents have been let down by Conservative-run Suffolk County Council.

If re-elected, I will keep working hard every day to stand up for our community, speak up for residents, and make sure Holywells ward gets the attention and investment it deserves.

My name is Stefan Long. I was born in Ipswich and have lived here my whole life. At age 16 I started an engineering apprenticeship at the Port of Felixstowe and have worked there since. From 2014 to 2020 I was a senior shop steward for Unite the union. I was elected to this role by my colleagues to represent their views and opinions on current events within the workplace and feedback to them about ongoing issues or updates on policy.

If elected as your local councillor my promise to you is simple. I will listen and engage with the local community. I will take on board your views and represent them where appropriate. I will keep you updated on proposed changes and explain to the best of my ability how and why decisions were made.

This is our town and residents within it deserve to have a voice and be heard.

Sue has lived in Ipswich since 2015, and before that lived in Barham. She is a retired adminstrator and has three grandchildren. She became really concerned about what we are doing to our children and grandchildren's future in 2019 and became an activist within the climate movement. She is a keen cyclist and would really like to see improvements in the cycle infrastructure in Ipswich. She is part of the Oak Tree community farm.

"Recently I have started a weekly Climate Cafe with my friend Tina, and we meet with others who are concerned about the Climate and Ecological Emergency to support each other.  I am pleased to be able to stand in Whitehouse and give the residents in that Ward an opportunity to vote for the change that is so desperately needed."

Lucy's professional background is in town planning - working as a planning officer for three London local authorities before moving to Ipswich in 1988 to raise her family, and then as a government Planning Inspector, deciding planning appeals. This has given her a good understanding of many aspects of local government.
Since retirement she has been involved with several community initiatives in Ipswich including taking a leading role in major restoration project at St Margaret's Church, volunteering at the Ipswich Winter Night Shelter, and as a Director of The Oak Tree, a community farm at Rushmere.
From her first exposure to politics as a teenager delivering Liberal Party (as was) leaflets with her father, Lucy has strongly believed that for individuals and communities to thrive the Liberal Democrat priorities of a fair society with a good environment and high quality public services responsive to local needs are fundamental.

Lucy says "If elected I would represent my constituents in challenging the majority party and administration where they fail to meet those objectives."

It has been a privilege to serve as councillor for Sprites, especially as I’m part of the local Chantry community myself, having lived here for over a decade. It is with pride that I’ve been able to represent the area on the Council.

I will continue to highlight the condition of many of our neglected roads. Through the local press and my position on the Scrutiny Committee, I have drawn the attention of Suffolk County Council to my report on our roads. Although some progress is being made, particularly on Sheldrake Drive, there is still much to be done.

In the last two years, I have been Chair of Labour councillors on Ipswich Borough Council and have overseen an immensely tough period for local government funding. I’m proud that we have protected frontline services, continue to provide affordable housing and invest in our town.

MIKE SCANES – A STRONG VOICE FOR RESIDENTS IN SPRITES   Mike has lived in Chantry since he moved from Manchester in 1985. Following his retirement from industry, he joined the NHS and worked for the Cancer Networks in East Anglia. He was part author of a guide for Carers of Cancer Patients published by a Major Cancer Charity. Mike was a supporter on the Hospice Movement both locally and Nationally. He was a member of the Patient and Public Involvement Forum for Suffolk Mental Health Trust. Through his involvement with the NHS and the Voluntary Sector, Mike is aware of the needs of families in getting quick access to services and will work tirelessly with the local NHS to achieve this goal. Mike who was widowed in 2004, has two daughters who both live in Ipswich, and two grandchildren who attend local schools. “As a  Grandad of school age children I am aware of the dangers of speeding traffic and unsafe parking in the school safety zones and the roads in Ipswich in general. I will work with the police and schools to make access to schools safer.” “Many of the roads in Chantry are in a very poor states with many pot holes, particularly Hawthorn Drive. There has been a deterioration over the last twelve months with no repair work being carried out. I will press the County Council to repair these roads before next winter”. In November 2017, Mike was diagnosed wit type 2 diabetes and with the help of the NHS and “One Life Suffolk” he has lost 30 kilos in weight and is now in remission from Diabetes. Please feel free to contact Mike YOUR PRIORITIES ARE MY PRIORITIES ROAD SAFETY AND SPEEDING  Speeding in our community puts lives at risk. The state of the roads with dangerous pot holes also puts lives at risk. We will work with the County Council and Tim Passmore, the Police and Crime Commissioner to make roads safer for the residents. We will push for long overdue road repairs in the area. SUPPORTING THE NHS IN IPSWICH Mike’s connection with the NHS means that he understan

Robin has lived in Ipswich for all but 2 of his 54 years. He joined the party in 1984 whilst at college and after graduating worked for the party as organiser until the 1987 general election.

From 1992-1994 he was Business Manager for Liberal Democrat News. He has worked as Political Assistant to to the Liberal Democrat Leaders of Chelmsford, Norwich and Isle of Wight and served as agent to Sir Bob Russell when he was MP for Colchester. He has also been a candidate or agent in elections in Ipswich many times.

He is currently a Royal Mail postman, part time researcher and supporting artist. He has been married to Hilary for over 30 years.

I’ve lived in Ipswich all my working life and used to work at BT Adastral Park. My family is very settled in the East side of town. I have represented the St Johns ward since the year 2000 and became Leader of Ipswich Borough Council in 2023.

I’m proud of the work that the Borough has done recently: building new council houses, bringing Jamacia Blue and Lovisa to the town centre, providing new play areas, refurbishing the Regent, bringing the first Portal to the UK, converting the former sugar beet site to an Industrial Park and being part of the City of Culture bid. I want to carry on and do more to give pride back to Ipswich.

Kirsty has lived in Ipswich for 13 years, including 10 years in St Margaret’s ward. After a decade studying environmental politics and working in Brussels and Westminster, she moved into the charity sector in Ipswich, advising community groups and small businesses on governance and energy efficiency. Since having children Kirsty has retrained in environmental education and child development. She now works as a forest and beach school leader for the local wildlife trust and as a pastoral support practitioner at St Margaret’s Primary School.

"I stand as a Green Party candidate to protect our natural environment and encourage fairness and kindness in our society. If elected I would fight against air pollution, town centre congestion, fuel poverty and inequality. I would champion community spaces, sustainable housing, and the local green economy. I‘d advocate for the protection of children’s well-being and access to nature for every child."
Barry Broom was born in Ipswich, his family has a long history here, he works here and he has started a family here and is fully committed to the town.

John is a computer programmer, and is married with two children.  He has been a Green party member for 12 years.  John was born in Ipswich and lives here with many family members who live in Ipswich also. He attends the Ipswich Quaker meeting, has been a school governer, and has worked as a volunteer at the winter homeless shelter as well as helping at the Suffolk LGBT Network.

"The old political parties don't understand the new challenges we face in the 21st century, we need Green policies locally and nationally to leave a world for our children and grandchildren".

I have lived in Ipswich since early childhood residing within the Ward I now represent. Running my own business for 40 years, I understand the ongoing trials and tribulations of managing day-to-day survival. As a Christian and connected to the church since the age of eleven when I became a choirboy and bellringer I believe passionately in fairness,  openness, accountability and transparency, especially in local government. If I am elected I would strive to be the voice of the residents within my Ward.

Martin recently retired after 24 years as Suffolk County Council's Outdoor Education Officer. In this role he advised and supported local schools on all aspects of school trips and adventure activities for their pupils. He is proud that during this time very many Suffolk young people were able to benefit from these opportunities. In retirement Martin volunteered as a trustee of the Thorpe Woodlands Adventure Centre which provide outdoor learning opportunities for young people.

Martin's wife, Elena, also now retired, worked as a lecturer at Essex University.

Working at the County Council Martin learned about how Councils work and how to get things done. He believes a Councillor's job is to listen to the concerns of local people and make the Council work for them. That's the Liberal Democrat way.

Martin writes "My wife and I have lived in central Ipswich for nearly 30 years. We now live in Valley Road close to the junction with Norwich Road and Chevallier Street. I am very aware of the traffic congestion in this area and share residents concerns about air quality. We need to look very carefully at how to tackle these problems before further new housing is built in Ipswich that would make things worse".

MARK PHILLIPS – A STRONG VOICE FOR RESIDENTS IN ST. JOHN’S
Mark has worked with vulnerable adults for the last nine years in residential care settings. He grew up locally off Woodbridge Road, was educated at St. John’s Primary School, and worshipped at St. John’s Church, Cauldwell Hall Road – before embarking on a university degree in London.
He now regularly attends the United Reformed Church in Cowper Street and the Salvation Army in Woodbridge Road. Mark used to help his late father in his signwriting business across the area.
Mark says “My brother Edward has been a local councillor for the last five years, and I have helped him in his campaigning work for local residents. So I am very familiar with the important role that local Councillors have in confronting the issues that local residents face. Prior to my brother being elected in Bixley Ward, he was a regular activist and candidate here in St John’s Ward, which finally deserves the return of a Conservative councillor for the first time in many years.
I would be honoured to receive your vote and become your new representative.” Mark has done voluntary work including helping the hospital chaplaincy service since 2007. He put himself forward to be elected as a Foundation Governor for the Woodlands Unit, at the Suffolk and Norfolk NHS Foundation Trust.
YOUR PRIORITIES ARE MY PRIORITIES
EMPOWERING LOCAL GROUPS AND CHURCHES 
We have lots of community groups and churches in St John’s Ward, and by attending some of them, Mark understands the important community work they do. Mark says “Councils can do nothing on their own. Working with these groups, together, we can help vulnerable and lonely people in the area.”
TACKLING HOSPITAL PARKING 
Working with neighbouring Councillors in Bixley, Mark will try to address ongoing issue of hospital parking problems in our streets, to make it easier for residents to park in their own road. Mark will join these other Councillors in their regular meetings with hospital ma

Born in Suffolk Trevor joined what was then the Liberal Party in 1983 having decided it was necessary to campaign for a realistic alternative to Margaret Thatcher's Conservatives and a fairer voting system. He subsequently got involved in local politics where he lived in Essex and served for eight years as a Liberal Democrat District Councillor from 1991 to 1999 including a spell as majority group leader. Trevor subsequently moved to Scotland and had a period campaigning for Charles Kennedy as he lived in his constituency. He is now back in East Anglia and living in Ipswich. He has previously stood for election to Ipswich Borough Council in Bixley ward.

Prior to the first lockdown Trevor did voluntary work with a youth club and a literacy charity. He currently supports IP3 Good Neighbours.
Trevor says "I am a great believer in policies being based on evidence rather than ideology. In my view the Liberal Democrats are the best party at offering this and have a proud record of helping elect some of the most hard working MP's and councillors in the Country."

I am your parliamentary candidate for Central Suffolk and North Ipswich.  My name is Tony Gould and I have been active in politics since 2015 when I first stood for UKIP in the local elections in Ipswich.  I then managed the Ipswich Brexit referendum for Vote Leave in 2016.  I also stood for UKIP in the GE in 2017, for the Brexit Party in the GE in 2019 and also as MEP in 2019.  I now need your help for the vitally important General Election later this year.

My background is in science and technology having spent my career at the British Telecom research establishment at Martlesham Heath.  I am now retired and my focus is trying to contribute to rectifying the dire state of this country after coping with some of the hopeless government policies over the last couple of decades. 

I have a Christian, conventional outlook and believe in integrity and hard work. Reform UK is the only party that reflects my views on immigration, education, the net zero problem and the Covid shambles, all of which have helped to bankrupt Britain.  I was a Conservative ward councillor in Ipswich, with the main purpose of opposing our Labour controlled Borough Council, but resigned recently to take up this mission.

It has been a privilege to represent the Gainsborough Ward since first being elected in 2016.  I have enjoyed working closely with the staff at the Gainsborough Library where such a variety of excellent events have taken place in the last 5 years.  I was also pleased to help fund some of the important work that has been done by the same staff who delivered food parcels to residents throughout the first lockdown.  Working with the BMX Club on Landseer Park to help ensure they can secure the funding needed to build their new clubhouse and refurbish their main track has been extremely rewarding particularly as they are now virtually there.  I am greatly looking forward to being able to meet up with everybody again in person when the Covid restrictions are finally lifted.
